titles.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Kicks a Woman in Her Private Part, and She Claims That She No Longer Menstruates
Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Kicks a Woman in Her Private Part, and She Claims That She No Longer Menstruates

segments: رَجُلٍ رَكَلَ اِمْرَأَةً فِي فَرْجِهَا فَزَعَمَتْ أَنَّهَا لاَ تَحِيضُ وَ كَانَ طَمْثُهَا مُسْتَقِيماً قَالَ "يُتَرَبَّصُ بِهَا سَنَةً فَإِنْ رَجَعَ إِلَيْهَا اَلطَّمْثُ وَ إِلاَّ غُرِّمَ اَلرَّجُلُ ثُلُثَ دِيَتِهَا لِفَسَادِ طَمْثِهَا وَ عَقْرِ رَحِمِهَا".
arabic.text: رَجُلٍ رَكَلَ اِمْرَأَةً فِي فَرْجِهَا فَزَعَمَتْ أَنَّهَا لاَ تَحِيضُ وَ كَانَ طَمْثُهَا مُسْتَقِيماً قَالَ "يُتَرَبَّصُ بِهَا سَنَةً فَإِنْ رَجَعَ إِلَيْهَا اَلطَّمْثُ وَ إِلاَّ غُرِّمَ اَلرَّجُلُ ثُلُثَ دِيَتِهَا لِفَسَادِ طَمْثِهَا وَ عَقْرِ رَحِمِهَا".

segments:
Hadith.5334 - Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a man who kicked a woman in her private area, and she claimed that her menstruation had stopped while it was previously regular.
Imam (as) said: "She should be observed for one year. If her menstruation returns, then nothing is required. But if it does not return, the man must pay one-third of her blood money (diyyah) for the damage to her menstruation and the harm caused to her womb."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.5334 - Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a man who kicked a woman in her private area, and she claimed that her menstruation had stopped while it was previously regular.
Imam (as) said: "She should be observed for one year. If her menstruation returns, then nothing is required. But if it does not return, the man must pay one-third of her blood money (diyyah) for the damage to her menstruation and the harm caused to her womb."

narratorChain.raw: 5334 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ رِجَالِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي

titles.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Kicks a Woman in Her Private Part, and She Claims That She No Longer Menstruates

segments: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَا تَرَى فِي رَجُلٍ ضَرَبَ اِمْرَأَةً شَابَّةً عَلَى بَطْنِهَا فَعَقَرَ رَحِمَهَا وَ أَفْسَدَ طَمْثَهَا وَ ذَكَرَتْ أَنَّهُ قَدِ اِرْتَفَعَ طَمْثُهَا عَنْهَا لِذَلِكَ وَ قَدْ كَانَ طَمْثُهَا مُسْتَقِيماً قَالَ "يُنْتَظَرُ بِهَا سَنَةً فَإِنْ صَلَحَ رَحِمُهَا وَ عَادَ طَمْثُهَا إِلَى مَا كَانَ وَ إِلاَّ اُسْتُحْلِفَتْ وَ أُغْرِمَ ضَارِبُهَا ثُلُثَ دِيَتِهَا لِفَسَادِ رَحِمِهَا وَ اِرْتِفَاعِ طَمْثِهَا".
arabic.text: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَا تَرَى فِي رَجُلٍ ضَرَبَ اِمْرَأَةً شَابَّةً عَلَى بَطْنِهَا فَعَقَرَ رَحِمَهَا وَ أَفْسَدَ طَمْثَهَا وَ ذَكَرَتْ أَنَّهُ قَدِ اِرْتَفَعَ طَمْثُهَا عَنْهَا لِذَلِكَ وَ قَدْ كَانَ طَمْثُهَا مُسْتَقِيماً قَالَ "يُنْتَظَرُ بِهَا سَنَةً فَإِنْ صَلَحَ رَحِمُهَا وَ عَادَ طَمْثُهَا إِلَى مَا كَانَ وَ إِلاَّ اُسْتُحْلِفَتْ وَ أُغْرِمَ ضَارِبُهَا ثُلُثَ دِيَتِهَا لِفَسَادِ رَحِمِهَا وَ اِرْتِفَاعِ طَمْثِهَا".

segments:
Hadith.5335 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"What is your ruling regarding a man who struck a young woman on her abdomen, causing damage to her womb and disrupting her menstruation? She reported that her menstruation had ceased due to this injury, whereas it had previously been regular."
Imam (as) said: "She should be observed for one year. If her womb recovers and her menstruation returns to normal, then there is no liability. But if it does not return, she is to take an oath, and the assailant must pay one-third of her diyyah (blood money) for the damage to her womb and the cessation of her menstruation."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.5335 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"What is your ruling regarding a man who struck a young woman on her abdomen, causing damage to her womb and disrupting her menstruation? She reported that her menstruation had ceased due to this injury, whereas it had previously been regular."
Imam (as) said: "She should be observed for one year. If her womb recovers and her menstruation returns to normal, then there is no liability. But if it does not return, she is to take an oath, and the assailant must pay one-third of her diyyah (blood money) for the damage to her womb and the cessation of her menstruation."

narratorChain.raw: 5335 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ
